title: Vertical AI agents: Why they'll replace SaaS and how to stay relevant
full_text:
Join our 60-minute workshop to supercharge your AI agents with actionable performance insights.
Register now
Solutions
Resources
Sign In Book a Demo
Back to blog
Contents
Table of content Item
It’s 2025, and the biggest trend in enterprise tech is the shift toward custom AI. Rather than using one-size-fits-all models, companies are looking for systems built to handle their unique needs–from automated workflows to tailor-made insights. As a result, many organizations are rethinking what AI can do for them.
This shift has led to the rise of vertical AI agents–AI tools focused on a single domain or function, designed to tackle that area’s specific challenges. Though they’re still relatively new, many industry insiders believe these specialized agents could someday rival or even replace traditional SaaS platforms. By zeroing in on a narrow set of tasks, they can deliver more precise results than any general-purpose AI. In what follows, we’ll explore the origins of vertical AI agents, why they’re generating so much buzz, and how SuperAnnotate can help build the data needed to evaluate AI agents effectively.
What are vertical AI agents?
Vertical AI agents are specialized AI systems designed to handle specific tasks or workflows in a single domain. Unlike broad, general-purpose models, they focus on a narrower set of challenges. Many experts see them as the next evolution of Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) , with organizations like Y Combinator even "rebranding" B2B SaaS to vertical AI agents.
The phrase “vertical AI agent” emerged because recent AI advancements have made building high-quality software significantly easier. Companies like Writer, Agentic, and Sierra have raised billions of dollars offering solutions that let people create their own B2B SaaS—now commonly referred to as vertical AI agents.
Throwback to SaaS
In a recent Y Combinator discussion on “The Light Cone,” the hosts compared the evolution of Vertical AI to the rise of Software as a Service (SaaS). SaaS started its momentum after the introduction of AJAX , especially the XML HTTP request in 2004. This technology allowed web browsers to perform complex tasks, previously only possible with desktop applications. It marked a major shift, taking software from individual computers to the cloud, which opened the door for the SaaS boom.
This shift is similar to how vertical AI agents are beginning to transform specific industry tasks with specialized AI solutions today. Just as SaaS created many successful companies by meeting general software needs, vertical AI could do even more by providing targeted solutions for niche business problems.
Vertical AI agents' impact
Vertical AI agents are set to have a huge impact on the market, potentially creating companies worth over $300 billion. They’re designed to target specific business operations, much like how SaaS products have revolutionized businesses in the past. However, the potential with vertical AI agents is even greater—they could be ten times larger than the traditional SaaS companies they replace, as Y Combinator states.
This massive scale is possible because vertical AI agents can streamline operations so effectively that they not only replace existing software but also reduce the need for large teams, thus cutting down on labor costs and increasing efficiency. For example, a company called Rippling is using vertical AI to develop a comprehensive suite of HR tools. If successful, it could outcompete several billion-dollar SaaS companies by consolidating multiple business functions into a single, more efficient platform.
In simple terms, vertical AI agents can potentially create immensely valuable new enterprises that operate with greater efficiency and at a larger scale than ever before.
Fine-tuning vertical AI agents
Vertical AI agents are, at their core, exceptionally well fine-tuned systems. Customization forms the foundation of these solutions, and market leadership will belong to those who’re highly specialized for specific industries.
Success in fine-tuning depends primarily on super-relevant, top-quality data from proprietary sources. In modern enterprise AI, data remains the most valuable and expensive resource. Organizations need to tap into their private data in order to ensure their agents have deep expertice in their field.
SuperAnnotate: The data foundry for AI agents
Building enterprise-level vertical AI agents begins with choosing the right data partner. Creating training data for vertical AI agents requires a platform that can adapt precisely to your requirements and operate everything in one unified platform. Your data pipeline must handle your specific requirements while maintaining consistency at scale.
This is where SuperAnnotate comes in – the leading enterprise solution for building large-scale multimodal AI datasets. What sets us apart is that we provide the only fully customizable platform in the market, with advanced tools for automation, data exploration and analysis. Rather than managing multiple tools, you get everything needed to evaluate your AI agents in one unified solution.
SuperAnnotate for agentic workflows
SuperAnnotate’s customizable UI builder, advanced workflows, and automation capabilities can flexibly adapt to the needs of your enterprise agentic processes.
Build your own UI: Quickly create custom annotation interfaces with drag-and-drop components, then extend them with code to address your agents’ specific data requirements.
For all your projects: From small pilot programs to large-scale data operations, every UI and workflow runs on a single platform—ensuring consistency, scalability, and easy management.
Adapt as conditions change: As your agent’s workflows evolve, so can your annotation interface. Easily reconfigure or swap out steps to tackle new challenges and maintain efficiency.
Go beyond standard workflows: Construct exactly the workflow steps your projects demand—all in one place. This flexibility helps you keep agent-driven tasks both efficient and accurate across different domains.
From pilot to production: SuperAnnotate grows with you. Whether you’re testing out new agentic ideas or rolling out enterprise-wide data pipelines, our platform smoothly transitions from proof of concept to full-scale deployment.
Ready to turn your agents into experts with us? Let’s talk about your project!
Vertical AI agent industries
Vertical AI agents will introduce a huge shift in how industries deploy AI, a few of these industries being:
Healthcare
In healthcare, these agents can deeply integrate with electronic health records to streamline processes like appointment scheduling and patient data management. They can also support diagnostic processes by analyzing patient histories and diagnostic data, helping medical professionals get quicker, data-driven insights.
Finance
In finance, vertical AI agents can potentially automate complex compliance and risk management tasks. They use advanced algorithms to monitor transactions continuously and detect anomalies that may indicate fraud. These agents can also automate regulatory compliance reporting and potentially surpass manual processes in accuracy and timeliness.
Customer service
Customer service is another area where vertical AI agents are making strides. They can manage routine inquiries and transactions, providing personalized responses based on customer history. For example, an AI agent for an e-commerce platform can handle returns, track deliveries, and recommend products, enhancing the customer experience while freeing up human agents for more complex issues.
These examples hint at the potential of vertical AI agents to massively change operations across industries. Their ability to process vast amounts of domain-specific data and generate actionable insights is paving the way for smarter, more responsive business models. But the key here is to really make sure they’re well-trained on niche data and prepared to handle real-world tasks. Otherwise, we’ll be witnessing many more failed AI cases and “agentic hallucinations”.
Early vertical AI winners
Vertical AI agents are already used in companies to automate business operations. A few of the early winners are:
Agentic : Automates various business processes to enhance productivity and streamline operations.
Sierra : Specializes in data analysis and business intelligence, providing insights from large datasets for better decision-making.
Writer AI : Builds AI apps and workflows into business processes.
Thoughts on vertical AI
In the Y Combinator community , there's a lively discussion about the future of vertical AI agents and their potential to outshine traditional SaaS. Here's a straightforward summary of the main viewpoints:
Beyond traditional SaaS: Some members argue that while vertical AI agents are built on SaaS foundations, they represent a shift from tools we operate to 'partners' we collaborate with, changing our relationship with technology.
Market dynamics: There's a concern that as AI makes products easier to build, increased competition could drive prices down, potentially benefiting major infrastructure providers ("hyperscalers") the most.
Niche opportunities: Others highlight the value of developing AI agents for niche markets, leveraging specialized domain expertise to create solutions with significant impact.
Final remarks
Vertical AI agents signal a fundamental shift in business operations. Traditional SaaS brought enterprise software to the cloud, and now agents are pushing the boundaries further by introducing tailored automation across industries. As the technology matures, vertical AI agents will emerge wherever there's a need for domain-specific automation, creating new opportunities for innovation and efficiency.
This evolution depends on two key elements: identifying promising areas for specialization and building agents with high-quality, domain-specific data. Organizations that effectively blend industry expertise with AI capabilities stand to make the most significant impact in their respective fields.
The question isn't whether vertical AI agents will reshape enterprise technology – they already are. What remains to be seen is how they'll coexist with or replace traditional SaaS solutions, and which companies will define this next wave of AI-driven transformation.

Companies like Writer, Agentic, and Sierra have raised billions of dollars offering solutions that let people create their own B2B SaaS—now commonly referred to as vertical AI agents.
Throwback to SaaS
In a recent Y Combinator discussion on “The Light Cone,” the hosts compared the evolution of Vertical AI to the rise of Software as a Service (SaaS). SaaS started its momentum after the introduction of AJAX , especially the XML HTTP request in 2004. This technology allowed web browsers to perform complex tasks, previously only possible with desktop applications. It marked a major shift, taking software from individual computers to the cloud, which opened the door for the SaaS boom.
This shift is similar to how vertical AI agents are beginning to transform specific industry tasks with specialized AI solutions today. Just as SaaS created many successful companies by meeting general software needs, vertical AI could do even more by providing targeted solutions for niche business problems.
Vertical AI agents' impact
Vertical AI agents are set to have a huge impact on the market, potentially creating companies worth over $300 billion. They’re designed to target specific business operations, much like how SaaS products have revolutionized businesses in the past. However, the potential with vertical AI agents is even greater—they could be ten times larger than the traditional SaaS companies they replace, as Y Combinator states.
This massive scale is possible because vertical AI agents can streamline operations so effectively that they not only replace existing software but also reduce the need for large teams, thus cutting down on labor costs and increasing efficiency. For example, a company called Rippling is using vertical AI to develop a comprehensive suite of HR tools. If successful, it could outcompete several billion-dollar SaaS companies by consolidating multiple business functions into a single, more efficient platform.
In simple terms, vertical AI agents can potentially create immensely valuable new enterprises that operate with greater efficiency and at a larger scale than ever before.
Fine-tuning vertical AI agents
Vertical AI agents are, at their core, exceptionally well fine-tuned systems. Customization forms the foundation of these solutions, and market leadership will belong to those who’re highly specialized for specific industries.
Success in fine-tuning depends primarily on super-relevant, top-quality data from proprietary sources. In modern enterprise AI, data remains the most valuable and expensive resource. Organizations need to tap into their private data in order to ensure their agents have deep expertice in their field.
SuperAnnotate: The data foundry for AI agents
Building enterprise-level vertical AI agents begins with choosing the right data partner. Creating training data for vertical AI agents requires a platform that can adapt precisely to your requirements and operate everything in one unified platform. Your data pipeline must handle your specific requirements while maintaining consistency at scale.
This is where SuperAnnotate comes in – the leading enterprise solution for building large-scale multimodal AI datasets. Rather than managing multiple tools, you get everything needed to evaluate your AI agents in one unified solution.

Vertical AI agent industries
Vertical AI agents will introduce a huge shift in how industries deploy AI, a few of these industries being:
Healthcare
In healthcare, these agents can deeply integrate with electronic health records to streamline processes like appointment scheduling and patient data management. They can also support diagnostic processes by analyzing patient histories and diagnostic data, helping medical professionals get quicker, data-driven insights.
Finance
In finance, vertical AI agents can potentially automate complex compliance and risk management tasks. They use advanced algorithms to monitor transactions continuously and detect anomalies that may indicate fraud. These agents can also automate regulatory compliance reporting and potentially surpass manual processes in accuracy and timeliness.
Customer service
Customer service is another area where vertical AI agents are making strides. They can manage routine inquiries and transactions, providing personalized responses based on customer history. For example, an AI agent for an e-commerce platform can handle returns, track deliveries, and recommend products, enhancing the customer experience while freeing up human agents for more complex issues.
These examples hint at the potential of vertical AI agents to massively change operations across industries. Their ability to process vast amounts of domain-specific data and generate actionable insights is paving the way for smarter, more responsive business models. But the key here is to really make sure they’re well-trained on niche data and prepared to handle real-world tasks. Otherwise, we’ll be witnessing many more failed AI cases and “agentic hallucinations”.
Early vertical AI winners
Vertical AI agents are already used in companies to automate business operations. A few of the early winners are:
Agentic : Automates various business processes to enhance productivity and streamline operations.
Sierra : Specializes in data analysis and business intelligence, providing insights from large datasets for better decision-making.
Writer AI : Builds AI apps and workflows into business processes.
Thoughts on vertical AI
In the Y Combinator community , there's a lively discussion about the future of vertical AI agents and their potential to outshine traditional SaaS. Here's a straightforward summary of the main viewpoints:
Beyond traditional SaaS: Some members argue that while vertical AI agents are built on SaaS foundations, they represent a shift from tools we operate to 'partners' we collaborate with, changing our relationship with technology.
Market dynamics: There's a concern that as AI makes products easier to build, increased competition could drive prices down, potentially benefiting major infrastructure providers ("hyperscalers") the most.
Niche opportunities: Others highlight the value of developing AI agents for niche markets, leveraging specialized domain expertise to create solutions with significant impact.
Final remarks
Vertical AI agents signal a fundamental shift in business operations. Traditional SaaS brought enterprise software to the cloud, and now agents are pushing the boundaries further by introducing tailored automation across industries. As the technology matures, vertical AI agents will emerge wherever there's a need for domain-specific automation, creating new opportunities for innovation and efficiency.
This evolution depends on two key elements: identifying promising areas for specialization and building agents with high-quality, domain-specific data. Organizations that effectively blend industry expertise with AI capabilities stand to make the most significant impact in their respective fields.
The question isn't whether vertical AI agents will reshape enterprise technology – they already are. What remains to be seen is how they'll coexist with or replace traditional SaaS solutions, and which companies will define this next wave of AI-driven transformation.
